summary.brief: Cothran v. Jauregui. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: On June 30, 2021, Renee Jauregui (“Jauregui”) filed a complaint asserting a medical malpractice claim against Dr. Cothran.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Jauregui alleged that, during at least four of the appointments, she informed Dr. Cothran of a lump she had discovered in her breast.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In August 2019, Jauregui returned to Dr. Cothran for a follow up on the breast lump and subsequent testing revealed that the lump was breast cancer.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Court of Appeals, however, concluded that the trial court erred because it misinterpreted the continuing treatment rule.
evidence_index.speaker: Court of Appeals

evidence_index.text: For the foregoing reasons, we will reverse the decision of the Court of Appeals and reinstate the trial court’s decision sustaining Dr. Cothran’s plea in bar.
evidence_index.speaker: Supreme Court

evidence_index.text: Jauregui testified that she first informed Dr. Cothran of the lump during a May 2018 visit and that Dr. Cothran examined the lump and diagnosed it as a clogged milk duct.
evidence_index.speaker: Jauregui

evidence_index.text: Dr. Cothran testified that Jauregui never complained about the lump during any of the 2018 visits and that she never examined Jauregui’s breast.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Cothran

evidence_index.text: The trial court sustained Dr. Cothran’s plea in bar. It explained: In the case before me today there’s no medical treatment at all.
evidence_index.speaker: Trial court

evidence_index.text: The Court of Appeals explained that the trial court misinterpreted the continuing treatment rule to require the existence of a specific treatment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court of Appeals

evidence_index.text: Viewed in this context, we are persuaded by the trial court’s rationale that a substantial interruption occurred.
evidence_index.speaker: Supreme Court

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports discovering a lump in her breast during pregnancy and informed her OB/GYN during multiple visits.

focused_exam.breast: Patient reported a lump; examination findings not detailed in judgment

requestables.name: Breast Ultrasound
requestables.description: Ultrasound of the breast to evaluate the lump

reveal.clinical_significance: Could help differentiate between benign and malignant causes
reveal.decision_impact: Should have been considered to rule out malignancy

requestables.name: Mammogram
requestables.description: Mammogram to assess the breast lump

reveal.clinical_significance: Standard imaging for breast lump evaluation
reveal.decision_impact: Should have been considered to rule out malignancy

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with a breast lump during pregnancy

key_events.event: Patient reports breast lump to Dr. Cothran

timeline_phases.name: Follow-up and Diagnosis
timeline_phases.description: Patient returns for follow-up and is diagnosed with breast cancer

key_events.event: Patient returns with changes in breast lump

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient presents with a breast lump during pregnancy
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in managing the breast lump?

options.clinical_reasoning: Ultrasound is a non-invasive method to evaluate breast lumps, especially during pregnancy.
Reassurance without further investigation may delay diagnosis of serious conditions like cancer.

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant reassured patient and advised monitoring
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant believed the lump was a normal part of pregnancy

expected_action_court.description: Breast ultrasound should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence suggests imaging is necessary to rule out malignancy

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to investigate the lump with imaging delayed the diagnosis of breast cancer.
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Imaging should have been performed to evaluate the lump.
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care requires investigation of breast lumps to rule out cancer.

patient_outcome.description: Patient diagnosed with metastatic breast cancer

key_findings: Court found no continuous and uninterrupted course of examination or treatment
Statute of limitations barred the claim

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to order imaging for breast lump
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Imaging could have led to earlier diagnosis

factual_timeline.event: Patient reports breast lump to Dr. Cothran
Patient returns with changes in breast lump

allegations.allegation: Failure to order imaging for breast lump

court_findings.finding: No continuous and uninterrupted course of examination or treatment
court_findings.legal_significance: Statute of limitations barred the claim
